tools.title: American Epidemic Timeline
tools.summary: A cinematic timeline of major U.S.-linked epidemics and disease outbreaks, designed as a public-health memory machine.
tools.evidence_model: Outbreak ledger with source links, rights-checked archival visuals, uncertainty notes, and public-health turning points
tools.long_note: The timeline follows large epidemics, public-health turning points, and regional outbreaks where the evidence is strong enough to carry the story.

tools.title: Pathogen Atlas
tools.summary: An origin-and-spread atlas that links geography, uncertainty, papers, and prior writing instead of flattening pathogens into another story stream.
tools.evidence_model: Origin claims, route confidence, and linked papers
tools.long_note: The pathogen atlas runs as a dedicated dark atlas app while drawing from the curated epi-dossier atlas export.

tools.title: Maritime Disease Atlas
tools.summary: A map-first digital exhibit on shipboard disease ecology, ports, vectors, provisions, quarantine, violence, and the movement constraints of life at sea.
tools.evidence_model: Exhibit stations with routes, mechanisms, archival plates, source credits, and guided-tour narrative
tools.long_note: This is the main public-history exhibit in the atlas family: an exploratory disease ecology surface with guided-tour and presentation modes for later video adaptation.

tools.title: Revolutionary War Disease Atlas
tools.summary: A historical epidemiology section focused on colonial and Revolutionary-era disease ecology, troop movement, and the public-health constraints around the founding period.
tools.long_note: A structured historical atlas page connecting colonial disease ecology, military movement, smallpox, inoculation, and public-health constraints around the founding period.

tools.title: Viking Health Atlas
tools.summary: A Norse-world disease and health map that ties Greenland, Vinland, settlement ecology, and pre-Columbian contact arguments into one visual surface.
tools.evidence_model: Map-based historical synthesis plus linked essays
tools.long_note: A Norse-world health and disease map connecting Greenland, Vinland, settlement ecology, pre-Columbian contact arguments, and epidemic uncertainty.
